stimulus: One of the most influential jazz drummers of the mid-twentieth century, ______blank
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: Max Roach’s recordings reshaped the rhythmic vocabulary of modern jazz.
the rhythmic vocabulary of modern jazz was reshaped by Max Roach’s recordings.
recordings by Max Roach reshaped the rhythmic vocabulary of modern jazz.
Max Roach reshaped the rhythmic vocabulary of modern jazz through his recordings.

rationale:
Choice D is correct. The convention being tested is subject-modifier placement. This choice makes the proper noun “Max Roach” the subject of the sentence and places it immediately after the modifying phrase “one of the most influential jazz drummers of the mid-twentieth century,” clearly establishing that Max Roach—and not another noun—is being described.

Choices A, B, and C all result in dangling modifiers because the noun immediately following the modifying phrase (“Max Roach’s recordings,” “the rhythmic vocabulary,” or “recordings by Max Roach”) is not the influential jazz drummer being described.
